title: Gustavo Pérez-Arrobo
text:
Hi! I am a PhD candidate in Political Science at the University of Colorado Boulder. I am also a graduate student associate in the Program on International Development at CU Boulder’s Institute of Behavioral Science.
My main research interests are political institutions, political economy, democracy, political behavior and methodology. My research examines how economic and political dynamics shape democratic systems with a regional emphasis on Latin America. It develops novel explanations for why some democracies endure while others falter, integrating structural and behavioral perspectives to examine democratic representation and resilience using a multi-method approach and original evidence.
My primary teaching interests include comparative politics, political economy, methodology, and Latin American politics.
I also hold a master’s degree in Comparative Politics from the Latin American Faculty of Social Sciences (Ecuador) and a bachelor’s degree in Economics from the Pontifical Catholic University of Ecuador.
Some of my research has been published in Revista de Ciencia Política and Revista Española de Ciencia Política.
